title: Modules and Require Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of CommonJS (require/module.exports) vs ES Modules (import/export) and the role of built-in Node.js modules for organized systems.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy behind the 'CommonJS' module system in Node.js?
options: A system focused on synchronous modules that use require() and module.exports to share code across files.

questions.question: Which modern JavaScript keyword is used in ES Modules (ESM) to share code with other files?
Is it possible to use ES Modules (import/export) in modern versions of Node.js?
options: Yes, by using the .mjs file extension or adding 'type': 'module' to your package.json.
No, it's only for the browser.

questions.question: Which and/or what built-in Node.js module is commonly used to work with file and directory paths across different OS environments?
Does Node.js allow you to use millions of pre-built packages and libraries through its built-in modules?
options: No, pre-built packages are managed through NPM, not the built-in core modules.
Yes, by providing a robust ecosystem for managing, sharing, and versioning your code.
